ogTitle: Struck Oil vs Sturt: Climate Safety

heading: Struck Oil vs Sturt — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Struck Oil, Queensland and Sturt, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sturt has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Struck Oil (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Struck Oil or Sturt safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Struck Oil has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Sturt scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Struck Oil scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Sturt scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Struck Oil or Sturt?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sturt has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
